title: Riot API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Riot Games API key detected in source code. Riot Games provides APIs for accessing data from League of Legends, Valorant, Teamfight Tactics, Legends of Runeterra, and other Riot Games titles. These API keys authenticate programmatic access to Riot's Developer Portal APIs, granting the ability to retrieve match data, player statistics, champion information, leaderboards, tournament data, real-time game information, and more. Exposing this key allows attackers to consume your API rate limits causing service disruption to legitimate applications, access player data and match history potentially violating privacy expectations, scrape competitive intelligence and gameplay patterns for unauthorized analysis, abuse your key for commercial data harvesting violating Riot's terms of service resulting in permanent API access revocation and potential legal action, execute unauthorized queries against Riot's infrastructure consuming your quota, access tournament and esports data for betting or gambling applications (explicitly prohibited), collect and sell player statistics to third parties, and potentially expose your application's architecture and data usage patterns to competitors. IMMEDIATELY revoke this API key from your Riot Developer Portal at https://developer.riotgames.com/, generate a new replacement key with appropriate rate limits and restrictions, audit recent API usage in your developer dashboard for unauthorized requests or suspicious activity patterns, review application logs for unexpected API calls or data access, check for unusual spikes in API usage that could indicate key compromise, rotate keys regularly as a security best practice (every 90 days recommended for production keys), store keys securely using environment variables, secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault, GCP Secret Manager), or encrypted configuration files (never commit keys to version control or expose in client-side code), implement proper access controls and least privilege principles for key distribution, use separate development and production keys to limit blast radius of compromise, implement rate limiting and caching in your applications to minimize API calls, monitor API usage patterns and set up alerts for anomalous activity, comply with Riot's API Terms of Use including data retention policies and prohibited use cases, and ensure your application properly handles API errors and respects rate limits to maintain good standing with Riot Games Developer Relations.
